subject: Databases
title: Read Replicas for Scale
description: A quick, practical guide to Read Replicas for Scale.

hook.text: The primary database handles writes and reads. Adding a read replica doubles your read capacity.

buildup.text: A read replica streams changes from the primary and serves read queries. Writes still go to the primary.

discovery.text: Route dashboards, reports, and search queries to replicas. Reserve the primary for transactions and writes.

twist.text: Replicas have replication lag. A write on the primary may take milliseconds to appear on the replica.

climax.text: Most managed databases (RDS, Cloud SQL) create replicas with one click. Scaling reads has never been easier.

punchline.text: Read replicas: scale reads without touching the primary. Lag is the trade-off.

quiz.question: What is replication lag?
options: The delay between a write on the primary and its availability on the replica
The time it takes to create a new replica
The speed difference between SSD and HDD storage
